text.title: Adapting the pattern per delivery surface
text.summary: The phase sequence and prompt files are stable; per-surface differences (verify command, e2e tooling, deploy mechanism) are encoded in domain playbooks under `playbooks/` and in reference docs under `reference/`.

section.title: Playbook scope
section.p: A playbook is a short markdown file that records the per-surface choices for each phase: which build command verifies, which test runner exercises end-to-end, which mechanism deploys, which files are typically touched together (informing the wave planner). It does NOT redefine the phase order or the prompt-file contracts.
section.reference: Skeleton showing the canonical playbook shape.

list.item: Build command per repo: `make`, `npm run build`, a project-specific build script, etc.
End-to-end runner: a browser automation framework for UI, a request runner for API, a job runner for ETL.
Deploy mechanism: CI pipeline trigger, container image push, helm upgrade, package publish, template upload script.
Local environment idiosyncrasies: hosts file entries, self-signed certs, mock data sources.
Code-style focus areas in review (e.g., framework-specific anti-patterns, type-system strictness).

section.title: Reference docs
section.p: Cross-cutting knowledge that more than one playbook needs goes under `reference/`:

list.item: `reference/git-conventions.md` — branch flow, commit format, push policy.
`reference/tracker-conventions.md` — chosen task manager, status names, ID format, MCP/CLI access.
`reference/delivery-channel.md` — chosen messaging delivery channel, credentials lookup, message format limits.
`reference/screenshot-strategy.md` — focused capture technique, outline colours, fallback for headless-only environments.

section.title: When a new playbook is justified

list.item: A new delivery surface enters the workflow (new repo, new artifact type).
Existing playbook would need more than two phase-specific overrides — split instead of bloating.
A new domain has materially different verify/test/deploy mechanics.

section.p: If only one or two phases differ, prefer a small note in the existing playbook over a new file.
